subscription_cost_note: CLI reports total_cost_usd=3.12; subscription-billed per D-012, recorded not paid

claims.description: Applying the header-shape heuristic per-line during add_data means any single malformed header line (e.g. 'Subject : hi', an mbox 'From ' line, a line missing its colon) terminates the header block early, silently reclassifying all subsequent legitimate headers as body and removing them from transaction.header; previously the heuristic ran only in end_data for messages that never sent a blank-line separator, so those headers were still parsed.

Dropping the '&& this.header_lines.length' condition makes end_data act on completely empty DATA, writing a spurious CRLF into the message stream and calling body.parse_end() on a Body that was never fed, which invokes banner/body-filter callbacks via _empty_filter() on an empty message.

The busted-email heuristic now runs per-line during streaming instead of only in end_data when no separator was ever seen, so a single malformed line inside an otherwise well-formed header block (UTF-8 BOM prefix, mbox 'From ' line, 'X Bad: value') prematurely ends the header block: every subsequent header is never passed to header.parse and is instead emitted as body text, whereas header.parse previously tolerated such lines and parsed the rest.

end_data's separator synthesis lost the old `&& this.header_lines.length` guard, so a transaction that received no DATA lines at all now calls header.parse([]) and writes a bare CRLF into message_stream, turning a genuinely empty message into a 2-byte one.

Header lines are now stored in message_stream dot-stuffed, whereas the old code reassigned `line` to the dot-stripped buffer before add_line, so a header line beginning with '.' is persisted with an extra leading dot that message_stream's remove_dot_stuffing (which requires a '..' prefix) will not strip.
